title: [lichen_util.c:20] lichen_sha256 output buffer size unchecked
description:
Contract: lichen_sha256 declares output[TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE] but this decays to pointer.
Violation: The function writes 32 bytes to output via tc_sha256_final() without runtime validation that the buffer is large enough. The header comment warns about this (lines 114-116 of lichen_util.h) but there is no runtime defense.
Impact: Buffer overflow if caller passes buffer < 32 bytes. This is called from generate_eui64() and lichen_pubkey_to_iid() which use correctly-sized local buffers, but any future caller could trigger overflow.
Mitigation: Document more prominently, or add explicit length parameter for defense-in-depth (would change API).
close_reason: No change needed: existing documentation already comprehensive. Header has SECURITY comment (lines 109-121) warning about array decay, 32-byte requirement, UB risk, and usage guidance. BUILD_ASSERT verifies digest size. Both callers (lora_l2.c, ipv6_addr.c) correctly use TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE. This is an accepted C limitation with adequate caller contract documentation.
